differential_diagnoses: Medication-induced delirium (e.g., due to benzodiazepine withdrawal or polypharmacy)
next_steps: Continuous video-EEG monitoring to differentiate ictal/postictal events from interictal psychosis
Perform lumbar puncture for cerebrospinal fluid analysis, including autoimmune encephalitis panel
Comprehensive metabolic panel and serum levels for all antiseizure medications
Review brain MRI for subtle structural changes or signs of autoimmune processes
Safety management with close observation and controlled reduction of potentially deliriogenic medications
Consultation with psychiatry for management of severe agitation and paranoid features
Neuropsychological evaluation to assess for cognitive or psychiatric dysfunction

differential_diagnoses: Delirium due to polypharmacy and medication withdrawal
next_steps: Perform lumbar puncture for CSF analysis, including autoimmune encephalitis antibody panel and paraneoplastic panel
Send serum autoimmune encephalitis antibody panel
Perform whole-body imaging (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis or PET-CT) to screen for occult malignancy
Continuous video-EEG monitoring to confirm if behavioral episodes represent ictal, postictal, or interictal activity
Carefully titrate or discontinue potentially deliriogenic medications (e.g., antipsychotics, benzodiazepines) under close supervision
Monitor for further autonomic instability and provide supportive ICU-level care if necessary
Obtain collateral history from family members to determine the timeline of personality and psychiatric changes
